title: Rivian Q3 Earnings Preview - Analyst Worries About Demand
text: Rivian Automotive is set to report third-quarter financial results Thursday after market close. The company is expected to announce revenue of approximately $1.00 billion, marking its lowest quarterly revenue in six quarters and a significant decline from last year's $1.34 billion. JPMorgan analyst Ryan Brinkman, who maintains an Underweight rating, is focused on demand weakness. He noted that the softer trend to production (and possibly deliveries also) could endanger the company's still growth stock multiple. The analyst lowered his price target from $14 to $12. Rivian recently reduced its annual production guidance to 47,000-49,000 vehicles from 57,000, citing component shortages. Despite this, the company maintained delivery guidance at 50,000-52,000 units. However, inventory additions in Q3 reached 3,139 units—significantly higher than the typical 950-unit quarterly average—potentially signaling softer demand. As one of the first automotive companies reporting post-2024 election, Rivian may face questions about how the Trump administration's policies could affect EV demand and the company's strategic direction. RIVN shares were trading at $10.20, up 5.07%, but remain down 53% year-to-date.
